assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a terminal with cURL.
No Box Free Developer account or developer token exists yet.
No language SDK is required for the compact first call.

developer_goal: Create a Box free developer account, confirm email, generate a developer token from the pre-built app, and complete an authenticated GET /users/me over HTTPS.

first_success.outcome: GET https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me with a Bearer developer token returns the authenticated console user JSON.
first_success.observable_signal: `curl https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me -H "authorization: Bearer [DEVELOPER_TOKEN]"` returns HTTP 200 and a user JSON object with fields such as `id`, `type`, `name`, and `login`.

route_selection.surface: Box Quick Start developer token and Get Current User via cURL
route_selection.rule: Use Quick Start for signup, email activation, pre-built app, and Generate Developer Token; prefer the Developer Tokens curl GET /users/me over heavier SDK upload-and-list tabs when the same credential reaches authenticated API success.
route_selection.selected: Quick Start signup + developer token + curl GET /users/me

candidates.name: Quick Start signup + developer token + curl GET /users/me
candidates.reason: Quick Start defines free signup, email confirmation, pre-built app, and Generate Developer Token. Developer Tokens documents curl GET /users/me as the minimal authenticated call with that token.

candidates.name: Quick Start SDK upload file and list root folder
candidates.reason: Official Quick Start demo, but requires SDK install and a sample file; same developer token can authenticate curl /users/me with fewer tooling steps.

candidates.name: Free Developer Plan default CCG token exchange
candidates.reason: Free Developer Plan still mentions automatic CCG app creation, but current Quick Start centers Generate Developer Token rather than a CCG client-credentials exchange.

candidates.name: Postman or Box CLI quick starts
candidates.reason: Separate tooling routes on the broader quick starts index.

required_path.action: Open Quick Start and complete Free Developer signup (name, email, password, terms, Get Started).
required_path.observable_result: Signup is submitted and the account awaits or proceeds to activation.

required_path.action: Confirm the email address to activate the developer account.

required_fields.label: Email confirmation
required_fields.notes: Quick Start requires confirmation; Free Developer Plan also says the account activates immediately.

required_path.observable_result: The developer account is activated and Developer Console is reachable.

required_path.action: Open the pre-built application in Developer Console and select Generate Developer Token.
required_path.observable_result: A developer token value is available to copy.

required_path.action: GET https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me with Authorization Bearer developer token via cURL and confirm user JSON.
required_path.observable_result: HTTP 200 user JSON for the authenticated console user.

prerequisites.description: Box Free Developer account with name, email, password, and terms acceptance.
Email confirmation to activate per Quick Start.
Developer token from the pre-built application (expires in one hour).
A terminal with cURL or equivalent HTTP client; no SDK required for the compact path.

external_gates.description: Email confirmation is required to activate the developer account on Quick Start.
Developer tokens expire after one hour and cannot be refreshed programmatically.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email confirmation delivery when activating the new developer account.

platform_outcomes.description: Box accepts GET /users/me with a valid developer token and returns the authenticated console user.

excluded.item: SDK upload and list root folder items
excluded.reason: Quick Start SDK tabs are heavier peers; curl /users/me uses the same developer token.
Current Quick Start centers Generate Developer Token on the pre-built app.

excluded.item: Custom first application, Postman, CLI, and production auth
excluded.reason: Follow after first authenticated API success.

uncertainties.question: Does Free Developer Plan 'activates immediately' override Quick Start email confirmation for some accounts?
uncertainties.impact: Email confirmation may be skippable or already satisfied depending on account state, which changes the required action count.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated fresh-account walkthrough comparing Quick Start activation copy with live signup and inbox confirmation.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup UI fields appear beyond name, email, password, and terms?
uncertainties.impact: Without a dated live signup capture, field counts stay unverified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Read-only walkthrough screenshots of the live developer signup form.
